title: POST a JSON pair and extract the sum
prompt: Read three lines from standard input: a URL, an integer a, and an integer b (each line is newline-terminated; trailing whitespace allowed on the digit lines). Issue an HTTP POST request to that URL with a 5000 ms transport timeout, header `Content-Type: application/json`, and JSON body `{"a":<a>,"b":<b>}` (no spaces around the colons or commas). On a transport-successful HTTP 200 response with a JSON body containing an integer `sum` field, write that integer as decimal followed by a newline, e.g. `7\n`. On any failure (transport error, non-200 status, JSON parse error, `sum` field missing or non-integer), write the literal string `error\n` to standard output. Do not write to standard error. Exit with status 0 in every case. Integers a, b, and sum fit in i32.

output_schema.stdout: either `<decimal_sum>\n` on a successful round-trip or `error\n` on any failure

notes_input_convention: Zero reads URL from argv[1], a from argv[2], b from argv[3] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability; the values are interpreted identically to the three stdin lines other languages read (trailing whitespace trimmed). All other languages read three newline-terminated lines from stdin. The verifier spins up a local HTTP fixture server before each language run and tears it down after; the fixture serves `/sum` (returns `{"sum":a+b}`), `/missing` (returns `{"other":99}`), `/badjson` (returns `not-json`), and `/404` (returns 404). The verifier passes inputs via stdin for non-Zero refs and via argv for Zero.
